title: Modular Architecture
objectives: Understand modular architecture via a real-life analogy
See how one repo splits into many pub packages
Know when modular is worth the ceremony (rarely for solo devs, essential at 5+ teams)

sections.markdown:
**Think of modular architecture as splitting one giant apartment building into a set of independent townhouses.**

- A **single-package Flutter app** is one big apartment building вЂ” all residents share the same lobby, elevators, plumbing, wiring. Everyone knows everything. Renovate the plumbing and everyone loses water for a week.
- A **modular app** is many townhouses side by side. Each has its own front door, plumbing, wiring. Residents share only shared amenities (park, sidewalks). Renovate ONE townhouse and the others don't notice.

**In code:** modular architecture splits your `lib/` into MANY Dart packages, each with its own `pubspec.yaml`, `lib/`, `test/`. They live under a monorepo (managed by `melos`). Each package has a strict public API (what it exports); everything else is private.

**Why teams reach for this:** at 5+ engineers on a single-package app, everyone's changes conflict. Compile times balloon. Code reviews touch every folder. Testing becomes 'let me run the whole app'. Splitting into packages FORCES the discipline that feature-first started вЂ” you literally CAN'T import from another package without declaring the dependency.

**When it's overkill:** solo developer, small team, sub-100k LOC. The ceremony (many pubspec files, melos setup, versioning) isn't worth it. **Layer-first в†’ feature-first в†’ modular is a natural progression as your app scales.**

**Modular architecture** splits a single Flutter app across multiple Dart packages inside a **monorepo**. Each package (e.g., `feature_auth`, `feature_cart`, `core_ui`, `data_api`) has its own `pubspec.yaml`, `lib/`, `test/`, versioning. The app's root `pubspec.yaml` pulls them via `path:` deps. Managed with **`melos`** вЂ” a tool for scripting cross-package operations (bootstrap, test, format). Enforces strong boundaries: no accidental imports, isolated tests, parallel CI, independent versioning.
## The three levels of module isolation

rows: Monorepo; each feature is a Dart package
Separate repos; published to pub / private registry

sections.markdown: ## Typical modular structure with `melos`

sections.code:
monorepo_root/

в”њв”Ђв”Ђ app/                          # the actual Flutter app
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ pubspec.yaml
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ lib/
в”‚       в””в”Ђв”Ђ main.dart             # wires all packages together

в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ core_ui/                  # shared widgets, theme

в”‚   в”‚       в””в”Ђв”Ђ core_ui.dart      # public API вЂ” `export` statements
в”‚   в”‚
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ core_services/            # AuthService, AnalyticsService, etc.
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ pubspec.yaml
в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ lib/
в”‚   в”‚
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ core_networking/          # shared Dio + interceptors
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ pubspec.yaml
в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ lib/
в”‚   в”‚
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ feature_auth/             # a feature
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ pubspec.yaml          # depends on core_services, core_ui
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ lib/

в”‚   в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ src/              # internal implementation
в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ test/                 # feature-scoped tests
в”‚   в”‚
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ feature_cart/
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ pubspec.yaml

в”‚   в”‚

в”‚   в”‚

в”‚
в””в”Ђв”Ђ tools/

# app/pubspec.yaml pulls features via path deps:
# dependencies:
#   feature_auth: { path: ../packages/feature_auth }
#   feature_cart: { path: ../packages/feature_cart }
#   ...
# feature_cart/pubspec.yaml can pull core_ui + core_services:
#   dependencies:
#     core_ui: { path: ../core_ui }
#     core_services: { path: ../core_services }
# feature_cart CANNOT depend on feature_auth вЂ” enforced by pubspec.

sections.markdown: ## `melos.yaml` вЂ” one place that scripts the whole monorepo

sections.code:
# melos.yaml at monorepo root
name: my_app_monorepo

packages:
  - app
  - packages/*

scripts:
  # `melos bootstrap` вЂ” install dependencies for all packages
  # Automatic: no config needed

  analyze:
    run: melos exec -- dart analyze
    description: Run dart analyze in every package.

  test:
    run: melos exec --dir-exists=test -- flutter test
    description: Run tests in every package that has a test/ folder.

  format:
    run: melos exec -- dart format --set-exit-if-changed .
    description: Format all Dart files.

  gen:
    run: melos exec -c 1 --depends-on=build_runner -- dart run build_runner build --delete-conflicting-outputs
    description: Run code generation across all packages that need it.

  clean:
    run: melos exec -- flutter clean

command:
  bootstrap:
    usePubspecOverrides: true    # dart 2.19+ pubspec overrides вЂ” cleaner than path: everywhere

# Common melos commands you'll actually run:

melos bootstrap             # link path deps, install everything
melos run analyze           # run dart analyze in every package
melos run test              # run tests in every package (or a subset)
melos run test --scope="feature_*"
melos run gen               # run build_runner in every package that needs it
melos publish               # publish selected packages
melos version               # bump versions across changed packages

sections.markdown: ## Common mistakes
**Mistake 1: Splitting into packages too early.** Solo dev on a 30-file app going modular = 3x the ceremony for no benefit. Stay feature-first until you have ~5 engineers or ~500 files.
**Mistake 2: Circular package dependencies.** `feature_cart` depends on `feature_auth` which depends on `feature_cart`. `melos bootstrap` will fail. Fix: extract shared parts to `core_*`.

**Mistake 3: Leaky public APIs.** Every package should have a clear public entry point (`lib/package_name.dart`) with `export` statements. Everything else lives in `lib/src/` and is effectively private. Import-from-`src/` breaks encapsulation.
**Mistake 4: Not scripting via `melos`.** Running `flutter test` in every package by hand doesn't scale. Set up `melos run test` early.

**Mistake 5: Independent versioning without a plan.** Bumping `feature_auth` from 1.0.0 to 2.0.0 while `feature_cart` stays at 1.0.0 requires thinking about compatibility. Most monorepos use SINGLE-VERSION for internal packages.
## Best practices
**1. Start with feature-first folders.** Migrate to modular only when the pain justifies it.
**2. Public API via `lib/package_name.dart`.** Everything internal in `lib/src/`.
**3. `core_*` packages for shared code.** `core_ui`, `core_services`, `core_networking`.
**4. Features cannot depend on other features.** Enforced by pubspec.
**5. Single version for internal packages.** Simpler than per-package semver at first.
**6. Set up `melos` from day 1 of modular.** Never run cross-package commands by hand.
**7. Parallel CI per package.** Test independently, aggregate results.
## Practice programs
**Practice 1 (Easy) вЂ” Set up a monorepo skeleton.** `app/` + `packages/core_ui/` + `packages/feature_home/`. Run `melos bootstrap`.
**Practice 2 (Medium) вЂ” Extract shared widgets.** Move `ProductCard` from your app into `core_ui`. Wire the dependency.
**Practice 3 (Medium) вЂ” Feature package.** Extract `features/auth/` into `packages/feature_auth/`. Verify it compiles.
**Practice 4 (Hard) вЂ” Enforce package boundaries.** Try to import `feature_cart` from `feature_auth`; watch pubspec fail.
**Practice 5 (Expert) вЂ” Parallel CI.** Set up GitHub Actions that tests each package in a separate job.
## Interview Q&A вЂ” with full answers you can quote

**Q1: What is modular architecture in Flutter?**

**A:** Modular architecture splits a single Flutter app across MULTIPLE Dart packages within a monorepo. Each package (`feature_auth`, `feature_cart`, `core_ui`, `core_services`) has its own `pubspec.yaml`, `lib/`, `test/`. The app's `pubspec.yaml` pulls them via `path:` dependencies. Managed with **`melos`** вЂ” a tool for scripting cross-package operations. **Why:** at 5+ engineers on a single-package app, everyone's changes conflict; compile times balloon; boundaries erode. Splitting into packages FORCES the discipline feature-first started вЂ” you literally can't accidentally import across features. **When:** medium-to-large apps, 5+ engineers, 500+ files. **Overkill for:** solo devs, small teams, tutorials.

**Q2: What is `melos` and why do you need it?**

**A:** `melos` is a Dart monorepo management tool from Invertase (creators of FlutterFire). It scripts cross-package operations: `melos bootstrap` links all path dependencies, `melos run test` runs tests in every package, `melos version` bumps versions across changed packages. Without `melos`, you'd manually `cd` into each package and run commands вЂ” unsustainable at 10+ packages. `melos` also supports **`melos.yaml` scripts** вЂ” reusable commands like `melos run gen` (run code generation across every package that has `build_runner`). Essential for any Dart monorepo. Modern Dart 3.5+ workspaces feature complements it.

**Q3: How does modular relate to feature-first?**

**A:** Modular is feature-first taken to the next level. **Feature-first (level 1):** one package, features organized by folder (`lib/features/auth/`). Discipline is directory-based; you can accidentally import across features. **Modular (level 2):** each feature is a SEPARATE PACKAGE. The compiler ENFORCES boundaries вЂ” you can't import from a package without adding it to your `pubspec.yaml`. Enforcement moves from lint to build system. **Migration path:** most teams start feature-first, promote to modular when the codebase hits ~500 files or the team crosses 5 engineers. The migration is mechanical: move folders to `packages/feature_*/lib/`, add pubspec files, use `melos bootstrap`.

**Q4: What are typical package types in a modular Flutter app?**

**A:** Three categories. **Feature packages (`feature_auth`, `feature_cart`)** вЂ” user-visible capabilities. Each has its own domain/data/presentation layers. Depends only on core packages. **Core packages (`core_ui`, `core_services`, `core_networking`, `core_theme`)** вЂ” shared infrastructure. `core_ui` has design-system widgets; `core_services` has AuthService etc.; `core_networking` provides a shared Dio configuration. Depend only on other core packages. **The app package** вЂ” wires all features together, sets up DI, provides the top-level router. Depends on everything. **Rule:** dependency arrows from features INTO core, from app INTO features + core. Never features в†’ features.

**Q5: How do you enforce package boundaries?**

**A:** Multiple mechanisms. **(1) `pubspec.yaml` deps** вЂ” `feature_cart`'s `pubspec.yaml` doesn't list `feature_auth` as a dependency, so imports fail at compile time. Strongest enforcement. **(2) Public API discipline** вЂ” `lib/feature_auth.dart` re-exports what's public via `export` statements; internal code lives in `lib/src/`. Consumers only see the public API. **(3) Dart's built-in `lib/src/` privacy** вЂ” files in `src/` shouldn't be imported directly by other packages (linters warn). **(4) `dart_code_metrics` custom rules** вЂ” flag imports from private paths. Together, these make cross-boundary drift nearly impossible.

**Q6: What are the trade-offs of modular architecture?**

**A:** **Pros:** (1) Enforced boundaries вЂ” impossible to accidentally cross features. (2) Faster CI вЂ” test packages in parallel. (3) Faster onboarding вЂ” new devs work in one package. (4) Independent versioning if needed. (5) Ability to extract packages to their own repo later. **Cons:** (1) More boilerplate вЂ” many `pubspec.yaml` files, many `lib/package_name.dart` public APIs. (2) `melos` learning curve. (3) Cross-package refactors are harder вЂ” 3 pubspec bumps + 3 test runs. (4) `path:` deps can be finicky (relative paths, IDE quirks). (5) Overkill for < 500 files or < 5 engineers. **My rule:** start feature-first; migrate to modular WHEN pain justifies it, not by anticipation.

**Q7: When is modular architecture worth it?**

**A:** **Worth it when:** (1) 5+ engineers touching the codebase daily. (2) 500+ files or 100+ screens. (3) Multiple features developed in parallel by separate teams. (4) Long build times (`flutter build` >2 minutes). (5) Test isolation matters (each feature can be tested in a fast, hermetic package). (6) You want to eventually extract features to their own repos. **Not worth it when:** (1) Solo developer. (2) Small team (2-3). (3) Under 500 files. (4) Fast, disposable prototypes. (5) The team doesn't know `melos` and doesn't have time to learn. **Migration path:** feature-first в†’ modular is a natural evolution; don't jump straight to modular on day 1.

## Real-world usage

- **Large enterprise Flutter apps** вЂ” banking, delivery, ride-share.
- **Google's Now In Android** вЂ” Kotlin/Compose reference; feature-modular structure.
- **BMW MyBMW app** вЂ” public case study, modular monorepo.
- **Flutter's own SDK repo** вЂ” internally modular.
- **FlutterFire monorepo** вЂ” 25+ Firebase plugin packages.
- **Melos itself is heavily used** in the Flutter ecosystem.
- **NOT common in indie / solo / small apps** вЂ” the ceremony isn't worth it.

## Advanced notes

- **Dart 3.5+ pubspec workspaces** вЂ” native workspaces feature; simplifies path deps.
- **`pubspec_overrides.yaml`** вЂ” Dart 2.19+ per-package overrides.
- **Private pub registries** вЂ” publish internal packages to a company registry (Cloudsmith, Verdaccio for Dart via API).
- **Package changelogs + versioning** вЂ” semver per package; `melos publish --dry-run` verifies.
- **Parallel `flutter build`** вЂ” CI can build feature-package tests in parallel across matrix jobs.
- **Nx / Bazel for very large scale** вЂ” cross-package caching; Google-scale.
- **Federated plugins (see Volume 4 packages)** вЂ” modular architecture within a plugin.
- **`melos run test --diff=main`** вЂ” test only changed packages (incremental CI).
- **Package graph visualization** вЂ” `melos list --graph` prints DOT graph of dependencies.
- **Golden test isolation** вЂ” each feature has its own golden test folder.
- **Refactoring across packages** вЂ” Dart's rename refactoring works, but slower; `find_and_replace_regex` or manual.

challenges.text: Set up a monorepo with app/ + packages/core_ui/ + packages/feature_home/.
melos bootstrap; verify all path deps resolved.
Extract ProductCard into core_ui; consume from feature_cart.
Convert an existing feature folder into a Dart package.
Add lib/package_name.dart public API; ensure lib/src/ is not imported directly.
Set up parallel CI: melos run test with matrix jobs per package.
Publish a core package to a private pub registry; consume it from the app.

What is melos and why do you need it?
How does modular relate to feature-first?
What are typical package types in a modular Flutter app?
How do you enforce package boundaries?
What are the trade-offs of modular architecture?
When is modular architecture worth it?
